name: Invoice Chaser
description: Tracks every outstanding invoice, sends polite escalating follow-ups on your behalf, and gives you a weekly cash-flow summary so you stop losing money to forgotten receivables.
author: ProwlrBot Team

before_after.before: Manually tracking 15+ invoices in a spreadsheet, writing awkward follow-up emails, discovering a $3,000 invoice is 60 days overdue
before_after.after: Every invoice tracked automatically with escalating follow-ups — you see a clean dashboard and only step in when the agent flags a problem

user_stories.quote: I'm a freelance designer and I used to lose track of who paid me. Last month this thing recovered $4,200 I'd basically forgotten about.
user_stories.name: Diana L.

user_stories.quote: The polite-but-firm follow-up emails are better than anything I'd write myself. My average payment time dropped from 38 days to 12.
user_stories.name: Raj P.

user_stories.quote: I hate confrontation. Having an agent handle overdue reminders means I maintain good client relationships and still get paid on time.
user_stories.name: Samantha W.

setup_steps.instruction: Connect your invoicing tool (QuickBooks, FreshBooks, Xero) or email account where you send invoices.
Set your follow-up schedule — how many days after the due date to send the first, second, and final reminder.
Choose the email tone: friendly and casual, professional and polite, or direct and firm.
Pick your weekly summary day and delivery channel (email, Slack, or console).
Optionally set a threshold amount for immediate alerts (e.g., notify me instantly for invoices over $1,000).
